description: Move WebSocket connection to start earlier by creating a WebSocketContext that connects once the user is authenticated, rather than waiting for Chat page mount. This ensures the WebSocket is already connected by the time the user navigates from Home to Chat.
steps: STEP 1 - Create a WebSocketContext (frontend/src/contexts/WebSocketContext.tsx) that manages the WebSocket connection lifecycle, generates/holds the sessionId, and provides connection state and sendQuery functionality to child components.
STEP 2 - Wrap the protected routes in App.tsx with the WebSocketProvider so the connection starts as soon as the user is authenticated and lands on any protected page (Home, Chat, etc.).
STEP 3 - Update Home.tsx to use the WebSocket context (generate sessionId via the context when submitting, pass it in the navigation state).
STEP 4 - Refactor Chat.tsx to consume the WebSocket context instead of managing its own connection. Keep the chat UI logic (messages, streaming, etc.) but remove the connection management code.
STEP 5 - Ensure reconnection logic still works (visibility change, network restore) by handling it in the context.
acceptance_criteria: TypeScript compiles without errors (npx tsc --noEmit passes)
Production build succeeds (npm run build passes)
WebSocket connection starts on the Home page (or any protected page) once user is authenticated
Chat page consumes the existing connection without creating a new one
Reconnection logic (visibility change, network restore) still works
Initial query auto-submit from Home page still works
Follow-up messages work after initial response completes
verification: Run `cd /projects/sandbox/wearcast/frontend && npx tsc --noEmit` and confirm it passes
Run `cd /projects/sandbox/wearcast/frontend && npm run build` and confirm production build succeeds
findings: Created WebSocketContext that manages connection lifecycle. WebSocketProvider wraps all protected routes via a ProtectedApp component in App.tsx. Home.tsx generates sessionId on mount which triggers early WebSocket connection. Chat.tsx refactored to consume the context. tsc --noEmit and npm run build both pass cleanly.
